cover.title: one agent is a demo. a second is an operating problem.
cover.sub: the moment two agents touch one repo, you have a coordination problem nobody demos.

slides.title: what breaks first
slides.body: two agents on one repo edit the same file and undo each other. no crash, no error. just silently lost work and a confused human.

slides.title: coordination is the unglamorous middle
items: how a human reads what the group did

slides.title: get it right
slides.body: and capacity scales with your seniors' judgment instead of fighting it. it's orchestration and ops, not prompting.

cta.title: coordinate agents without chaos
cta.sub: ownership, handoffs, and a legible trail. the boring parts that make a fleet actually work.
